 {"id":"franken_networkx-fyls","title":"parity: conversion live views should expose EdgeView.data() helper","description":"The top-level to_directed(...) and to_undirected(...) live views currently expose edge-view objects without NetworkX's .data() helper. -{"id":"franken_networkx-g04k","title":"parity: subgraph should preserve live edge attribute and in-subset edge updates","description":"Graph-family subgraph() currently snapshots edge state. After H = G.subgraph([...]), changing an included edge attribute like weight=9, or adding a new edge between already-included nodes, is immediately reflected by NetworkX subgraph views.  {"id":"franken_networkx-g4zs","title":"NX-REPLACE: Connectivity extensions — all_pairs_node_connectivity, minimum_st_node_cut, edge_disjoint_paths, all_node_cuts","description":"## Functions (6) — connectivity algorithm extensions
1. all_pairs_node_connectivity (__init__.py:3305) — {(u,v): connectivity} for all pairs
2. minimum_st_node_cut (__init__.py:3328) — min node cut between s and t (Rust impl exists)
3. minimum_edge_cut (__init__.py:1360) — min edge cut (Rust impl exists)
4. edge_disjoint_paths (__init__.py:5593) — decompose max flow into edge-disjoint paths
5. node_disjoint_paths (__init__.py:5600) — decompose flow into node-disjoint paths
6. all_node_cuts (__init__.py:5607) — enumerate all minimum node cuts

## Implementation plan
- Quick wins (existing Rust): minimum_st_node_cut, minimum_edge_cut — just rewire Python
- Medium: all_pairs_node_connectivity — loop over all pairs using existing node_connectivity
- Hard: edge_disjoint_paths, node_disjoint_paths — max flow decomposition into augmenting paths
- Hard: all_node_cuts — iterative cut enumeration

## Testing requirements
Create tests/python/test_connectivity_extensions_parity.py:
- test_all_pairs_node_conn_matches_nx: compare full dict on small graph (<=8 nodes)
- test_all_pairs_node_conn_disconnected: verify 0 for disconnected pairs
- test_minimum_st_node_cut_matches_nx: compare for 3+ (s,t) pairs
- test_minimum_edge_cut_matches_nx: compare cut value and set
- test_edge_disjoint_paths_count: verify number matches max_flow_value
- test_edge_disjoint_paths_are_disjoint: verify no shared edges
- test_edge_disjoint_paths_matches_nx: compare count against NX
- test_node_disjoint_paths_count: verify number matches node_connectivity
- test_node_disjoint_paths_are_disjoint: verify no shared internal nodes
- test_all_node_cuts_are_valid: each cut disconnects the graph
- test_all_node_cuts_are_minimum: each cut has size == node_connectivity

Run: pytest tests/python/test_connectivity_extensions_parity.py -v --tb=long

## Files to modify
- python/franken_networkx/__init__.py: rewire 2 quick-wins
- crates/fnx-algorithms/src/lib.rs: implement 4 new algorithms
- crates/fnx-python/src/algorithms.rs: add 4 bindings
- tests/python/test_connectivity_extensions_parity.py: new test file","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:19:34.902145520Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-04T00:19:38.669541243Z","closed_at":"2026-04-04T00:19:38.669267976Z","close_reason":"Safe connectivity tranche already landed; split remaining native all_node_cuts work into franken_networkx-3tgn","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -2741,6 +2741,38 @@ def test_graph_classes_subgraph_tracks_node_attr_mutations_like_networkx(fnx_cls
     assert _graph_snapshot(result) == _graph_snapshot(expected_result)
 
 
+@pytest.mark.parametrize(
+    ("fnx_cls", "nx_cls"),
+    [
+        (fnx.Graph, nx.Graph),
+        (fnx.DiGraph, nx.DiGraph),
+        (fnx.MultiGraph, nx.MultiGraph),
+        (fnx.MultiDiGraph, nx.MultiDiGraph),
+    ],
+)
+def test_graph_classes_subgraph_tracks_edge_mutations_like_networkx(fnx_cls, nx_cls):
+    graph, expected = _view_utility_graph_pair(fnx_cls, nx_cls)
+
+    result = graph.subgraph(["a", "b", "c"])
+    expected_result = expected.subgraph(["a", "b", "c"])
+
+    if graph.is_multigraph():
+        graph["a"]["b"][1]["weight"] = 9
+        expected["a"]["b"][1]["weight"] = 9
+        graph.add_edge("a", "c", key=7, weight=11)
+        expected.add_edge("a", "c", key=7, weight=11)
+    else:
+        graph["a"]["b"]["weight"] = 9
+        expected["a"]["b"]["weight"] = 9
+        graph.add_edge("a", "c", weight=11)
+        expected.add_edge("a", "c", weight=11)
+
+    assert result.is_directed() == expected_result.is_directed()
+    assert result.is_multigraph() == expected_result.is_multigraph()
+    assert fnx.is_frozen(result) == nx.is_frozen(expected_result)
+    assert _graph_snapshot(result) == _graph_snapshot(expected_result)
+
+
 @pytest.mark.parametrize("utility_name", ["subgraph", "induced_subgraph"])
 def test_induced_subgraph_helpers_missing_node_error_match_networkx_without_fallback(
     monkeypatch, utility_name
